title: VerificationPlan
description: The framework-NEUTRAL ordered verification plan a downstream command-plan SUPPLIER produces and T018 executes (FR-048); T018/T019 never discover or invent commands; an empty plan is the explicit verification-not-configured state. Commands run in DECLARED order (never sorted); each carries a stable command_id (unique within the plan) that binds its recorded evidence to the reviewed-tree digest. Arguments are a strict string ARRAY (shell behaviour must be an explicit interpreter invocation); working_directory/result_path must be repository-relative and are rejected if rooted or escaping via '..' or a symlink; timeout_seconds is bounded by engine policy (0/absent -> engine default, over-max clamped, never unlimited); provenance is an auditable object; and NO secret env values may appear (only env_refs NAMES).

schema_version.description: Contract version. Exactly '1.0' for this schema; a future shape bumps this.

commands.description: The ORDERED verification commands. Executed in declared order and NEVER sorted. An EMPTY array is the explicit verification-not-configured state (never a silent success). command_id values MUST be unique across the array.

command_id.description: Stable id, UNIQUE within the plan; recorded evidence joins on command_id + reviewed-tree digest.

executable.description: The program to run (resolved via PATH by the recorded-run runner). Framework-neutral: pytest, cargo, dotnet, bash, pwsh, or anything else.

arguments.description: A strict string ARRAY (default []). NEVER a single shell string — shell behaviour is an explicit interpreter invocation, e.g. executable 'pwsh' arguments ['-File', 'verify.ps1'] or executable 'bash' arguments ['-lc', 'make test'].

working_directory.description: OPTIONAL. Repository-RELATIVE + canonical. Rejected if rooted/absolute, if it escapes via '..', or if a symlink/junction resolves outside the repository root.

timeout_seconds.description: OPTIONAL per-command budget (default 0). Bounded by engine policy: 0/absent resolves to the engine DEFAULT (never unlimited); a value over the engine MAX is clamped. A supplier can never buy an unlimited run.

result_path.description: OPTIONAL. Repository-RELATIVE + canonical (same path-safety rules as working_directory). Where the command WRITES its SpecrewTestResult during the run so its counts gain evidence standing.

require_result.description: OPTIONAL (default false). When true the command MUST produce a schema-valid SpecrewTestResult at result_path; an absent/invalid result is a VERIFICATION FAILURE for that command (command_succeeded=false), never a richer clean claim.

provenance.description: AUDITABLE provenance object naming HOW the command entered the plan — not a bare enum.

kind.description: One of the four provenance kinds.

source.description: The config path / detection signal / profile name / provider id that produced this command.

provider.description: REQUIRED when kind='provider-gated' — the gating provider id.

profile.description: REQUIRED when kind='profile-selected' — the selected profile name.

env_refs.description: OPTIONAL allowlist of env var NAMES to pass through. NAMES ONLY — a literal 'env'/'environment' value map is forbidden (no secret values may appear in a plan or its recorded evidence). AUTHORITATIVE (2026-07-14): the child environment is constructed from an EMPTY map + the engine's normative platform baseline (Get-ContinuousCoReviewVerificationEngineBaseline — currently EMPTY on Windows and Linux, every entry requiring paired runtime-evidence tests) + exactly these declared names, resolved from the ambient environment at spawn. HOME/USERPROFILE/APPDATA/LOCALAPPDATA are never implicit; PSModulePath/locale/terminal/tool variables must be declared here. The executable is resolved to a full path BEFORE the environment is constructed, so PATH is not implicitly required.

label.description: OPTIONAL human-readable label for the command.
